behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is in wiring across multiple source clients and the aggregation handoff. A pure unit test with mocked return objects can miss misconfigured source adapters, missing field mapping, or dropped sections in integration glue.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report builder with three test doubles at module boundaries (metrics endpoint stub, issue tracker stub, deployment log fixture) and assert the exact payload sent into summary-generation contains all three source sections with expected keys/values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: The key failure boundary is cross-module error handling and fallback rendering when one adapter raises/returns failure. In-process unit tests can miss exception translation and section-level fallback logic in the assembled pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute report generation with two healthy source adapters and one adapter stub that times out/errors. Verify process completes, output report contains a `data unavailable` marker only for that section, and other sections are still populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on semantic faithfulness of generated language, which is model-output quality and can vary run-to-run. Mocking or deterministic stubs would hide hallucination, omission, or value distortion failures.
behaviors.test_description: Use real summary-generation model calls on a fixed dataset with known key facts, run multiple trials, and score outputs for factual inclusion/accuracy against required assertions (e.g., exact critical values and trend direction). Fail if factual error/omission rate exceeds threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This crosses real network/service boundaries (source APIs + email transport + inbox delivery). Mocks can pass while real SMTP/API auth, MIME formatting, spam filtering, or recipient routing fails.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled script in an environment with reachable test source endpoints and a real test mailbox. Verify the email is received by configured recipients, subject/body/attachment format is intact, and report content corresponds to collected run data.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary includes network outage behavior plus filesystem persistence across runs and scheduler-triggered retry. Mocking delivery can hide real connection errors, file permission/path issues, or retry state not surviving process boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Force real delivery endpoint failure (e.g., blocked mail host) during a scheduled run; assert report is written to configured local fallback path. On next scheduled execution with email service restored, verify saved report is retried and successfully delivered, with retry state cleared.
